section_title: CDD for Legal Persons, Enhanced Measures, and Periodic Updation

content_blocks.text:
## 11. CDD for Legal Persons, Enhanced Measures, and Periodic Updation

### 11.1 CDD Measures for Legal Persons (Other Than Natural Persons)

The Company's customer base is currently restricted to individual retail clients. The Company shall not onboard legal-person clients (companies, partnerships, trusts, NPOs, etc.) without prior Board approval and documented expansion of the Company's CDD framework to address legal-person CDD requirements under Paragraph 4.3 of the 2026 Guidelines and Rule 9 of the PML Rules.

Where such expansion is approved by the Board, the legal-person CDD framework shall include all elements specified under Paragraphs 4.3.1, 4.3.2, and 4.3.3 of the 2026 Guidelines, including PAN verification, beneficial ownership identification under Rule 9(3) of the PML Rules, and DARPAN portal verification for Non-Profit Organisation clients.

#### 11.2.4 Inability to Apply Enhanced Measures

In accordance with Paragraph 4.4.2 of the 2026 Guidelines and Rule 10(3) of the PML Rules, in cases where the Company is not able to undertake the required enhanced measures under CDD, the Company shall:

(a) Terminate the business relationship; and

(b) File a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) with FIU-IND in accordance with Section 15 of this Policy.

The decision to terminate the relationship shall be taken with senior management approval. The reasons for termination and the STR filing shall be documented and retained as part of the Company's records.

### 11.3 Periodic CDD Updation

#### 11.3.1 Risk-Sensitive Updation (Paragraph 4.5.1)

Given the high-risk nature of Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs), the Company shall undertake periodic updation of CDD on existing clients.

Such periodic review shall form part of the Company's risk-based approach (RBA) framework. The review shall include necessary verification or enhanced measures under CDD where material changes in ownership, business, or transaction behaviour are observed.

#### 11.3.2 Periodicity of KYC Updation (Paragraph 4.5.2)

Under the risk-based approach, the Company shall ensure that all Client Due Diligence information and data remain accurate, up to date, and relevant.

**For clients assessed as High Risk:** KYC updation shall be carried out **at least once every six (6) months**.

**For all other clients:** KYC updation shall be carried out **at least once every year**, measured from the date of account opening or the last KYC updation, whichever is later.

#### 11.3.3 Documented Policy and Priority Handling (Paragraph 4.5.3)

The policy governing periodic KYC updation shall be clearly documented within the Company's CDD program and shall be duly approved by the Board.

For clients in respect of whom periodic updation of KYC has already fallen due, the Company shall ensure that the accounts of such clients are subject to KYC updation on a priority basis.

#### 11.3.4 Updation Procedure by Scenario

In accordance with Paragraph 4.5.3 of the 2026 Guidelines:

**(a) No change in CDD information (Paragraph 4.5.3(a)):**

In case there is no change in the CDD information of the client, the Company shall obtain a self-declaration from the client to that effect. The Company shall also ensure that the Beneficial Ownership (BO) information available with it, in case of legal persons, remains accurate and up to date. A self-declaration in this regard shall be obtained from the client.

**(b) Change in CDD information (Paragraph 4.5.3(b)):**

In case of any change in CDD information, the Company shall undertake the CDD process equivalent to that applicable for on-boarding a new client, including verification.

**(c) Expired CDD documents (Paragraph 4.5.3(c)):**

The Company shall ensure that the validity of CDD documents available with it has not expired. In such cases, the Company shall undertake the CDD process equivalent to that applicable for on-boarding a new client.

**(d) Records of updation (Paragraph 4.5.3(d)):**

The Company shall ensure that information and documents obtained from clients at the time of updation or periodic updation of KYC are promptly updated in the Company's records and database. A record mentioning the date of updation of CDD details shall also be maintained.

**(e) Additional and exceptional measures (Paragraph 4.5.3(e)):**

Any additional and exceptional measures adopted by the Company beyond the above (such as the requirement of obtaining a live selfie/video, requirement of physical presence of the client, or more frequent periodicity of KYC updation) shall be clearly specified in the Company's CDD program.

#### 11.3.5 Client Obligation to Intimate Changes (Paragraph 4.5.4)

The Company shall advise its clients that, in order to comply with the PML Rules, in case of any update in the information or documents submitted by the client at the time of establishing the relationship, the client shall promptly intimate and submit to the Company the updated information or documents.

This advisory shall be communicated to the client at the time of onboarding, prominently displayed in the public summary of this Policy under Section 6.4 of this Policy, and reinforced periodically through Company communications.
